name: SpectrumAnalyzer
author: Nguyen
description: Runs spectrum analysis on the whole library, album by album: detects fake lossless / lossy transcodes via frequency-cutoff heuristics and stores a spectrogram (base64 PNG) per song. Tracks are analyzed once and re-analyzed only when the underlying file changes; results are removed automatically by the core cleanup task.

versions.changelog: Deep analyze button per track: scans the ENTIRE file (chunked) and tracks the spectral edge over time — an edge that follows the music downgrades UPSAMPLED/FAKE_SUSPECT to a likely genuine dark master, a constant wall raises confidence. Manual 'verified' checkbox per track: verified tracks are excluded from all suspect counts, shown as an overview column, and filterable ('only albums with verified tracks').

Fix: album names containing &, ?, # or other URL-special characters broke the album page link (query string was built without percent-encoding). All album links and pagination URLs are now properly URL-encoded.

Fake 24-bit detection: PCM samples are probed for zero-padded low bits (new UPSCALED verdict, effective/container bits stored and shown per track). UPSAMPLED confidence is now corroborated by aliasing-image analysis: content above the cutoff that mirrors the band below it is a resampler artifact, not a dark master.

UPSAMPLED detection now matches the cutoff against every standard rate's Nyquist (22.05/24/44.1/48 kHz), so 48 kHz->96 kHz upsamples (content stopping at 24 kHz) are caught, not just 44.1 kHz sources. Confidence is graded by edge sharpness and shelf silence.

New UPSAMPLED verdict for fake hi-res: a >48 kHz container whose content stops at CD bandwidth (with digital silence above) is flagged as a resampled 44.1/48 kHz source instead of CLEAN. UPSAMPLED counts as suspect in the overview filters.

Parallel scans: one task per album fanned out across all workers, with a parent task tracking done/remaining. Fewer false FAKE_SUSPECT verdicts: content reaching ~20.5 kHz counts as full bandwidth, and a sharp edge with audible noise above the cutoff is now LOWPASSED, not FAKE. UI: filter albums by suspect count, re-analyze a whole album from its page.

Initial release: library scan grouped by album, change detection (metadata fingerprint + audio MD5), fake-lossless verdicts, base64 spectrograms, per-track re-analysis, on_song_analyzed hook, optional weekly incremental cron.
